41: function batchedInQuery<T>(db: Database.Database, sql: string, ids: (number | string)[], batchSize = 500): T[] {
42:   const results: T[] = [];
43:   for (let i = 0; i < ids.length; i += batchSize) {
44:     const batch = ids.slice(i, i + batchSize);
45:     const placeholders = batch.map(() => '?').join(', ');
46:     const batchSql = sql.replace('__PLACEHOLDERS__', placeholders);
47:     results.push(...(db.prepare(batchSql).all(...batch) as T[]));
48:   }
49:   return results;
50: }

52: function dedupeRowsById<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(rows: T[]): T[] {
53:   const results: T[] = [];
54:   const seenIds = new Set<number | string>();
55: 
56:   for (const row of rows) {
57:     const id = row.id;
58:     if (typeof id === 'number' || typeof id === 'string') {
59:       if (seenIds.has(id)) {
60:         continue;
61:       }
62:       seenIds.add(id);
63:     }
64:     results.push(row);
65:   }
66: 
67:   return results;
68: }

403: // Validate the decompressed checkpoint snapshot before
404: // restore. Previously the gzipped blob was JSON.parsed and cast directly
405: // to CheckpointSnapshot — malformed memory_index rows propagated through
406: // `getMemoryIds()` and the INSERT loops. This schema validates the
407: // outer envelope and the array shapes; individual row shapes are still
408: // permissive (sqlite columns evolve over time) but each row must be a
409: // non-null object so downstream column extraction doesn't NPE.
410: const CheckpointTableSnapshotSchema = z.object({
411:   columns: z.array(z.string()),
412:   rows: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())),
413: });
414: 
415: const CheckpointVectorRowSchema = z.object({
416:   rowid: z.number(),
417:   embedding: z.unknown(), // Buffer or null — preserved as-is
418: });
419: 
420: const CheckpointSnapshotSchema = z.object({
421:   manifest: z.object({
422:     snapshot: z.array(z.string()),
423:     rebuild: z.array(z.string()),
424:     skip: z.array(z.string()),
425:   }).optional(),
426:   tables: z.record(z.string(), CheckpointTableSnapshotSchema).optional(),
427:   memories: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())),
428:   workingMemory: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())),
429:   vectors: z.array(CheckpointVectorRowSchema).optional(),
430:   causalEdges: z.array(z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())).optional(),
431:   timestamp: z.string(),
432: }).passthrough();

468: function getErrorCode(error: unknown): string | null {
469:   if (typeof error === 'object' && error !== null && 'code' in error) {
470:     const code = (error as { code?: unknown }).code;
471:     return typeof code === 'string' ? code : null;
472:   }
473:   return null;
474: }

476: function isCheckpointBusyError(error: unknown): boolean {
477:   const code = getErrorCode(error);
478:   const message = toErrorMessage(error);
479:   return code === 'SQLITE_BUSY'
480:     || code === 'SQLITE_LOCKED'
481:     || /\bSQLITE_BUSY\b|\bSQLITE_LOCKED\b|database is locked/i.test(message);
482: }
483: 
484: function classifyCheckpointCreateError(
485:   error: unknown,
486:   context: Record<string, unknown> = {},
487: ): CheckpointCreateError {
488:   if (error instanceof CheckpointCreateError) {
489:     return error;
490:   }
491: 
492:   const originalCode = getErrorCode(error);
493:   const message = toErrorMessage(error);
494:   const details = {
495:     ...context,
496:     ...(originalCode ? { originalCode } : {}),
497:     originalMessage: message,
498:   };
499: 
500:   if (originalCode === 'SQLITE_BUSY' || /\bSQLITE_BUSY\b|database is locked/i.test(message)) {
501:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
502:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_SQLITE_BUSY',
503:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the SQLite database was busy. Retry after current writes finish.',
504:       details,
505:       error,
506:     );
507:   }
508: 
509:   if (originalCode === 'SQLITE_LOCKED' || /\bSQLITE_LOCKED\b/i.test(message)) {
510:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
511:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_SQLITE_LOCKED',
512:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the SQLite database was locked by another operation.',
513:       details,
514:       error,
515:     );
516:   }
517: 
518:   if (/UNIQUE constraint failed: checkpoints\.name|constraint failed/i.test(message)) {
519:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
520:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_DUPLICATE_NAME',
521:       'Checkpoint creation failed because a checkpoint with this name already exists.',
522:       details,
523:       error,
524:     );
525:   }
526: 
527:   if (
528:     originalCode === 'EACCES'
529:     || originalCode === 'EPERM'
530:     || originalCode === 'SQLITE_READONLY'
531:     || /permission denied|readonly|read-only/i.test(message)
532:   ) {
533:     return new CheckpointCreateError(
534:       'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_PERMISSION_DENIED',
535:       'Checkpoint creation failed because the database path is not writable.',
536:       details,
537:       error,
538:     );
539:   }
540: 
541:   return new CheckpointCreateError(
542:     'CHECKPOINT_CREATE_FAILED',
543:     `Checkpoint creation failed: ${message}`,
544:     details,
545:     error,
546:   );
547: }

684: function sweepOrphanCheckpointSnapshotDirs(database: Database.Database, checkpointsDir: string): void {
685:   // Checkpoint pruning removes the catalog row inside the create transaction but deletes
686:   // the snapshot dir AFTER commit. A crash in that gap leaves a snapshot dir with no
687:   // catalog row — never swept by the .tmp-* sweep (it has a published name) and never
688:   // restorable. Reconcile on-disk v2 snapshot dirs against the live catalog and remove
689:   // any that no catalog row references. Best-effort: never throw out of the create path.
690:   try {
691:     if (!fs.existsSync(checkpointsDir) || !tableExists(database, 'checkpoints')) {
692:       return;
693:     }
694: 
695:     const checkpointColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
696:     if (!checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_path')) {
697:       return;
698:     }
699:     const referenced = new Set<string>();
700:     const rows = database.prepare(
701:       "SELECT snapshot_path FROM checkpoints WHERE snapshot_path IS NOT NULL AND snapshot_path <> ''",
702:     ).all() as Array<{ snapshot_path?: string | null }>;
703:     for (const row of rows) {
704:       if (typeof row.snapshot_path === 'string' && row.snapshot_path.length > 0) {
705:         referenced.add(resolvePathSafe(row.snapshot_path));
706:       }
707:     }
708: 
709:     for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(checkpointsDir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
710:       if (!entry.isDirectory() || entry.name.includes('.tmp-')) {
711:         continue;
712:       }
713:       const entryPath = path.join(checkpointsDir, entry.name);
714:       if (referenced.has(resolvePathSafe(entryPath))) {
715:         continue;
716:       }
717:       try {
718:         fs.rmSync(entryPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
719:         console.warn(`[checkpoints] Swept orphan checkpoint snapshot dir with no catalog row: ${entry.name}`);
720:       } catch (_removeError: unknown) {
721:         // Best-effort: a single un-removable dir must not block checkpoint creation.
722:       }
723:     }
724:   } catch (error: unknown) {
725:     console.warn(`[checkpoints] Orphan checkpoint snapshot sweep skipped (non-fatal): ${toErrorMessage(error)}`);
726:   }
727: }

777: function loadCheckpointV2Manifest(snapshotPath: string | null): CheckpointV2Manifest {

780:       'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_MANIFEST_INVALID',
781:       'Checkpoint v2 restore failed because snapshot_path is missing.',
782:     );
783:   }
784: 
785:   const manifestPath = path.join(snapshotPath, 'manifest.json');
786:   try {
787:     const raw = fs.readFileSync(manifestPath, 'utf-8');
788:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<CheckpointV2Manifest>;
789:     if (
790:       parsed.formatVersion !== 2
791:       || typeof parsed.includeEmbeddings !== 'boolean'
792:       || !Array.isArray(parsed.mainTables)
793:       || !Array.isArray(parsed.vecTables)
794:       || typeof parsed.schemaVersion !== 'number'
795:       || typeof parsed.memoryCount !== 'number'
796:       || typeof parsed.vectorCount !== 'number'
797:     ) {
798:       throw new Error('manifest shape is invalid');
799:     }
800:     return parsed as CheckpointV2Manifest;
801:   } catch (error: unknown) {
802:     throw new CheckpointRestoreError(
803:       'CHECKPOINT_RESTORE_MANIFEST_INVALID',
804:       `Checkpoint v2 restore failed because manifest.json could not be read: ${toErrorMessage(error)}`,
805:       error,
806:     );
807:   }

836: function fsyncFileIfPossible(filePath: string): void {
837:   let fd: number | null = null;
838:   try {
839:     fd = fs.openSync(filePath, 'r');
840:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
841:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
842:     // Best-effort durability; the next boot still treats any visible journal as authoritative.
843:   } finally {
844:     if (fd !== null) {
845:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
846:     }
847:   }
848: }

850: function fsyncDirectoryIfPossible(dirPath: string): void {
851:   let fd: number | null = null;
852:   try {
853:     fd = fs.openSync(dirPath, 'r');
854:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
855:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
856:     // Directory fsync is unavailable on some platforms/filesystems.
857:   } finally {
858:     if (fd !== null) {
859:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
860:     }
861:   }

966: function mergeCheckpointCatalogRows(database: Database.Database, rows: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): void {
967:   if (rows.length === 0 || !tableExists(database, 'checkpoints')) {
968:     return;
969:   }
970: 
971:   const targetColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
972:   for (const row of rows) {
973:     const name = row.name;
974:     if (typeof name !== 'string' || name.length === 0) {
975:       continue;
976:     }
977:     const existingByName = database.prepare('SELECT id FROM checkpoints WHERE name = ?').get(name) as { id?: number } | undefined;
978:     if (existingByName?.id) {
979:       continue;
980:     }
981: 
982:     let columns = Object.keys(row).filter((column) => targetColumns.has(column));
983:     const id = row.id;
984:     if (typeof id === 'number') {
985:       const existingById = database.prepare('SELECT name FROM checkpoints WHERE id = ?').get(id) as { name?: string } | undefined;
986:       if (existingById?.name && existingById.name !== name) {
987:         columns = columns.filter((column) => column !== 'id');
988:       }
989:     }
990:     if (columns.length === 0) {
991:       continue;
992:     }
993: 
994:     const placeholders = columns.map(() => '?').join(', ');
995:     database.prepare(`
996:       INSERT OR IGNORE INTO checkpoints (${columns.join(', ')})
997:       VALUES (${placeholders})
998:     `).run(...columns.map((column) => row[column]));
999:   }
1000: }

1002: function statPathBytes(targetPath: string): number {
1003:   try {
1004:     const stats = fs.statSync(targetPath);
1005:     if (stats.isFile()) {
1006:       return stats.size;
1007:     }
1008:     if (!stats.isDirectory()) {
1009:       return 0;
1010:     }
1011:     return fs.readdirSync(targetPath).reduce((total, entry) => total + statPathBytes(path.join(targetPath, entry)), 0);
1012:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1013:     return 0;
1014:   }
1015: }

1035: function collectPrunedCheckpointSnapshotPaths(database: Database.Database, pruneCount: number): string[] {
1036:   if (pruneCount <= 0) {
1037:     return [];
1038:   }
1039:   const checkpointColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'checkpoints'));
1040:   const snapshotPathSelect = checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_path') ? 'snapshot_path' : 'NULL AS snapshot_path';
1041:   const snapshotFormatSelect = checkpointColumns.has('snapshot_format') ? 'snapshot_format' : "'v1' AS snapshot_format";
1042:   const rows = database.prepare(`
1043:     SELECT id, ${snapshotPathSelect}, ${snapshotFormatSelect}
1044:     FROM checkpoints
1045:     ORDER BY created_at ASC, id ASC
1046:     LIMIT ?
1047:   `).all(pruneCount) as Array<{ id: number; snapshot_path?: string | null; snapshot_format?: string | null }>;
1048: 
1049:   if (rows.length === 0) {
1050:     return [];
1051:   }
1052:   const ids = rows.map((row) => row.id);
1053:   const placeholders = ids.map(() => '?').join(', ');
1054:   database.prepare(`DELETE FROM checkpoints WHERE id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...ids);
1055:   return rows
1056:     .filter((row) => row.snapshot_format === 'v2' && typeof row.snapshot_path === 'string' && row.snapshot_path.length > 0)
1057:     .map((row) => row.snapshot_path as string);
1058: }

1077: function hasMainVectorPayloadTables(database: Database.Database): boolean {
1078:   try {
1079:     // Gate v2 selection on vec_memories ONLY — the actual vector payload that the
1080:     // shard-attach slimming removes from main. vec_metadata is a tiny key/value
1081:     // config table that the same slimming intentionally RETAINS in main as a
1082:     // dimension fallback, so its presence does NOT mean vector payload lives in
1083:     // main. Including it here made full-DB create always observe "payload in main"
1084:     // on a sharded runtime and silently fall back to the v1 whole-snapshot path
1085:     // that overflows V8's single-string ceiling — the exact failure v2 prevents.
1086:     const row = database.prepare(`
1087:       SELECT 1 AS found
1088:       FROM sqlite_master
1089:       WHERE type IN ('table', 'view')
1090:         AND name = 'vec_memories'
1091:       LIMIT 1
1092:     `).get() as { found?: number } | undefined;
1093:     return row?.found === 1;
1094:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1095:     return false;
1096:   }
1097: }

1107:         SELECT 1 AS found

1109:         WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = 'vec_memories'

1185: function toBuffer(value: unknown): Buffer | null {
1186:   if (Buffer.isBuffer(value)) return value;
1187:   if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
1188:     const maybeSerialized = value as { type?: unknown; data?: unknown };
1189:     if (
1190:       maybeSerialized.type === 'Buffer' &&
1191:       Array.isArray(maybeSerialized.data) &&
1192:       maybeSerialized.data.every((entry) => typeof entry === 'number')
1193:     ) {
1194:       return Buffer.from(maybeSerialized.data);
1195:     }
1196:   }
1197:   return null;
1198: }

1207: function getMemoryIds(memories: Array<Record<string, unknown>>): number[] {
1208:   const ids = new Set<number>();
1209:   for (const memory of memories) {
1210:     if (!memory || typeof memory !== 'object') {
1211:       continue;
1212:     }
1213:     const rawId = memory.id;
1214:     if (typeof rawId === 'number' && Number.isSafeInteger(rawId)) {
1215:       ids.add(rawId);
1216:       continue;
1217:     }
1218:     if (typeof rawId === 'string' && /^\d+$/.test(rawId)) {
1219:       ids.add(Number.parseInt(rawId, 10));
1220:     }
1221:   }
1222:   return Array.from(ids);
1223: }

1233:         ? parsed as Record<string, unknown>
1234:         : {};
1235:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
1236:       return {};
1237:     }
1238:   }
1239:   return typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value)
1240:     ? value as Record<string, unknown>

1252:     (normalizedScope.tenantId === undefined || metadata.tenantId === normalizedScope.tenantId)
1253:     && (normalizedScope.userId === undefined || metadata.userId === normalizedScope.userId)
1254:     && (normalizedScope.agentId === undefined || metadata.agentId === normalizedScope.agentId)

224:     END
225:   `);
226: 
227:   database.exec(`
228:     CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS memory_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON memory_index BEGIN
229:       INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts, rowid, title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text)
230:       VALUES ('delete', old.id, old.title, old.trigger_phrases, old.file_path, old.content_text);
231:     END
232:   `);
233: 
234:   database.exec(`INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')`);
235: }
236: 
237: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
238:    4. MAIN API
239: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
240: 
241: function downgradeSchemaV16ToV15(
242:   database: Database.Database,
243:   options: SchemaDowngradeOptions = {}
244: ): SchemaDowngradeResult {
245:   const currentVersion = getCurrentSchemaVersion(database);
246:   if (currentVersion !== TARGET_FROM_VERSION) {
247:     throw new Error(`Downgrade supports only v${TARGET_FROM_VERSION} -> v${TARGET_TO_VERSION}. Current schema is v${currentVersion}.`);
248:   }
249: 
250:   ensureColumnsExist(database, OLLAMA);
251: 
252:   const checkpointName = options.checkpointName || `pre-schema-downgrade-v16-to-v15-${nowTimestampForName()}`;
253:   const checkpoint = checkpoints.createCheckpoint({
254:     name: checkpointName,
255:     specFolder: options.specFolder || null,
256:     metadata: {
257:       reason: 'schema downgrade v16->v15',
258:       fromVersion: TARGET_FROM_VERSION,
259:       toVersion: TARGET_TO_VERSION,
260:       removedColumns: [...OLLAMA],
261:     },
262:   });
263: 
264:   if (!checkpoint) {
265:     throw new Error('Failed to create pre-downgrade checkpoint');
266:   }
267: 
268:   database.pragma('foreign_keys = OFF');
269:   try {
270:     const run = database.transaction(() => {
271:       database.exec(`
272:         DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS memory_fts_insert;
273:         DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS memory_fts_update;
274:         DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS memory_fts_delete;
275:         DROP TABLE IF EXISTS memory_fts;
276:       `);
277: 
278:       database.exec(`ALTER TABLE memory_index RENAME TO memory_index_v16_backup`);
279: 
280:       createMemoryIndexV15(database);
281: 
282:       const columnsCsv = V15_COLUMNS.join(', ');
283:       database.exec(`
284:         INSERT INTO memory_index (${columnsCsv})
285:         SELECT ${columnsCsv}
286:         FROM memory_index_v16_backup
287:       `);
288: 
289:       // Drop backup table to release index names before re-creating v15 indexes.
290:       database.exec(`DROP TABLE memory_index_v16_backup`);
291: 
292:       createV15Indexes(database);
293:       createFtsArtifacts(database);
294: 
295:       database.prepare(`
296:         INSERT OR REPLACE INTO schema_version (id, version, updated_at)
297:         VALUES (1, ?, datetime('now'))
298:       `).run(TARGET_TO_VERSION);
299:     });
300: 
301:     run();
302:   } finally {
303:     database.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
304:   }
305: 
306:   const row = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM memory_index').get() as { count: number };
307:   return {
308:     fromVersion: TARGET_FROM_VERSION,
309:     toVersion: TARGET_TO_VERSION,
310:     checkpointName: checkpointName,
311:     preservedRows: row.count,
312:     removedColumns: [...OLLAMA],
313:   };
314: }
315: 
316: export {
317:   downgradeSchemaV16ToV15,
318: };
319: 
320: /**
321:  * Re-exports related public types.
322:  */
323: export type {
324:   SchemaDowngradeOptions,
325:   SchemaDowngradeResult,
326: };

105: function hasTable(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {
106:   const row = database.prepare(
107:     "SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'table' AND name = ? LIMIT 1"
108:   ).get(tableName) as { present?: number } | undefined;
109:   return row?.present === 1;

111: 
112: function hasIndex(database: Database.Database, indexName: string): boolean {
113:   const row = database.prepare(
114:     "SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'index' AND name = ? LIMIT 1"
115:   ).get(indexName) as { present?: number } | undefined;
116:   return row?.present === 1;

119: /**
120:  * Tier/usage columns may be absent on legacy databases that predate the
121:  * corresponding migrations. Missing columns are selected as NULL so the
122:  * protection decision degrades conservatively instead of crashing the sweep.
123:  */
124: const OPTIONAL_RETENTION_COLUMNS: ReadonlyArray<{ column: string; alias: string }> = [
125:   { column: 'importance_tier', alias: 'importanceTier' },
126:   { column: 'decay_half_life_days', alias: 'decayHalfLifeDays' },
127:   { column: 'is_pinned', alias: 'isPinned' },
128:   { column: 'access_count', alias: 'accessCount' },
129:   { column: 'last_accessed', alias: 'lastAccessed' },
130: ];

132: function isSoftDeleteTombstonesEnabled(): boolean {
133:   return process.env.SPECKIT_SOFT_DELETE_TOMBSTONES?.trim().toLowerCase() === 'true';
134: }
135: 
136: function selectExpiredRows(database: Database.Database, useSoftDeleteTombstones: boolean): RetentionExpiredRow[] {
137:   const presentColumns = new Set(
138:     (database.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(memory_index)').all() as Array<{ name: string }>)
139:       .map((row) => row.name),
140:   );
141:   const hasDeletedAt = presentColumns.has('deleted_at');
142:   const usePurgeableIndex = useSoftDeleteTombstones && hasDeletedAt && hasIndex(database, 'idx_memory_purgeable_retention');
143:   const memoryIndexSource = usePurgeableIndex
144:     ? 'memory_index INDEXED BY idx_memory_purgeable_retention'
145:     : 'memory_index';
146:   const deletedAtSelect = hasDeletedAt ? 'deleted_at AS deletedAt' : 'NULL AS deletedAt';
147:   const tombstonePredicate = useSoftDeleteTombstones && hasDeletedAt ? 'AND deleted_at IS NOT NULL' : '';
148:   const optionalSelects = OPTIONAL_RETENTION_COLUMNS
149:     .map(({ column, alias }) => (presentColumns.has(column)
150:       ? `${column} AS ${alias}`
151:       : `NULL AS ${alias}`))
152:     .join(',\n      ');
153: 
154:   return database.prepare(`
155:     SELECT
156:       id,
157:       spec_folder AS specFolder,
158:       file_path AS filePath,
159:       content_hash AS contentHash,
160:       tenant_id AS tenantId,
161:       user_id AS userId,
162:       agent_id AS agentId,
163:       session_id AS sessionId,
164:       delete_after AS deleteAfter,
165:       ${deletedAtSelect},
166:       ${optionalSelects}
167:     FROM ${memoryIndexSource}
168:     WHERE delete_after IS NOT NULL
169:       ${tombstonePredicate}
170:       AND datetime(delete_after) < datetime('now')
171:     ORDER BY datetime(delete_after) ASC, id ASC
172:   `).all() as RetentionExpiredRow[];
173: }

175: /** Importance tiers that must never be deleted on TTL expiry alone. */
176: const PROTECTED_RETENTION_TIERS = new Set(['constitutional', 'critical']);
177: 
178: /**
179:  * Tier-aware deletion decision evaluated BEFORE the destructive delete path.
180:  * Constitutional/critical tiers and pinned rows are protected from TTL-only
181:  * deletion; a null or unknown tier falls back to the pre-existing behavior
182:  * (unprotected rows keep deleting) so legacy rows are handled without crashes.
183:  */
184: function isProtectedFromRetentionDelete(row: RetentionExpiredRow): boolean {
185:   const tier = typeof row.importanceTier === 'string'
186:     ? row.importanceTier.trim().toLowerCase()
187:     : null;
188:   if (tier !== null && PROTECTED_RETENTION_TIERS.has(tier)) {
189:     return true;

205:     FROM memory_index

210:     LIMIT 1

224:   database: Database.Database,
225:   candidateCount: number,

235:     usesPurgeablePartition,
236:     usingPurgeableIndex: usesPurgeablePartition && hasIndex(database, 'idx_memory_purgeable_retention'),
237:     candidateCount,

243:   database: Database.Database,
244:   candidates: RetentionExpiredRow[],

253:     FROM causal_edges
254:     WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders}) OR target_id IN (${placeholders})
255:   `).get(...ids, ...ids) as { count: number };
256:   return { causalEdgesTablePresent: true, candidateEdgeCount: row.count };
257: }

259: function buildFeedbackRetentionReport(
260:   database: Database.Database,
261:   candidates: RetentionExpiredRow[],

264: ): FeedbackRetentionSweepReport {

266:   const activeGatePassed = mode === 'active' && args.shadowEvaluationPassed === true;
267:   const signals = args.signals ?? (dryRun
268:     ? []
269:     : aggregateEvents(database, (args.runAt ?? Date.now()) - (args.windowMs ?? BATCH_WINDOW_MS), args.runAt ?? Date.now()));
270:   const evaluation = evaluateFeedbackRetention(candidates, signals, {
271:     runAt: args.runAt,
272:     extendDays: args.extendDays,
273:   });
274: 
275:   return {
276:     mode,
277:     activeGatePassed,
278:     activeBlocked: mode === 'active' && !activeGatePassed,
279:     auditCount: 0,
280:     decisions: evaluation.decisions,
281:     extendedIds: [],
282:     protectedIds: evaluation.decisions
283:       .filter((decision) => decision.decision === 'protect')
284:       .map((decision) => decision.memoryId),
285:     deletedIds: [],

287: }

289: function recordFeedbackRetentionAudits(
290:   database: Database.Database,
291:   candidates: RetentionExpiredRow[],
292:   report: FeedbackRetentionSweepReport,
293:   applied: boolean,
294: ): number {
295:   const rowsById = new Map(candidates.map((candidate) => [candidate.id, candidate]));
296:   let auditCount = 0;
297:   for (const decision of report.decisions) {
298:     const row = rowsById.get(decision.memoryId);
299:     if (!row) continue;
300:     recordFeedbackRetentionAudit(database, row, decision, {
301:       mode: report.mode,
302:       applied,
303:       activeGatePassed: report.activeGatePassed,
304:     });
305:     auditCount += 1;
306:   }

310: function appendRetentionLedger(
311:   database: Database.Database,
312:   deletedIds: number[],
313:   candidates: RetentionExpiredRow[],
314: ): boolean {
315:   try {
316:     mutationLedger.initLedger(database);
317:     mutationLedger.appendEntry(database, {
318:       mutation_type: 'delete',
319:       reason: `memory_retention_sweep: retention_expired ${deletedIds.length} memory row(s)`,
320:       prior_hash: null,
321:       new_hash: mutationLedger.computeHash(`retention-sweep:${deletedIds.join(',')}:${Date.now()}`),
322:       linked_memory_ids: deletedIds.slice(0, 50),
323:       decision_meta: {
324:         tool: 'memory_retention_sweep',
325:         reason: 'retention_expired',
326:         totalDeleted: deletedIds.length,
327:         originalDeleteAfter: candidates
328:           .filter((candidate) => deletedIds.includes(candidate.id))
329:           .map((candidate) => ({ id: candidate.id, deleteAfter: candidate.deleteAfter })),
330:       },
331:       actor: 'mcp:memory_retention_sweep',
332:     });
333:     return true;
334:   } catch (error: unknown) {
335:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
336:     console.warn(`[memory-retention-sweep] mutation ledger append failed: ${message}`);
337:     return false;
338:   }
339: }

341: function runPostDeleteMaintenance(database: Database.Database): void {
342:   try {
343:     database.prepare("INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('optimize')").run();
344:   } catch (error: unknown) {
345:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
346:     console.warn(`[memory-retention-sweep] FTS optimize skipped: ${message}`);
347:   }
348: 
349:   let shouldRunIncrementalVacuum = false;
350:   try {
351:     const autoVacuumMode = database.pragma('auto_vacuum', { simple: true });
352:     shouldRunIncrementalVacuum = Number(autoVacuumMode) === 2;
353:   } catch (error: unknown) {
354:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
355:     console.warn(`[memory-retention-sweep] auto_vacuum check skipped: ${message}`);
356:   }
357: 
358:   if (shouldRunIncrementalVacuum) {
359:     try {
360:       database.pragma('incremental_vacuum');
361:     } catch (error: unknown) {
362:       const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
363:       console.warn(`[memory-retention-sweep] incremental vacuum skipped: ${message}`);
364:     }
365:   }
366: 
367:   try {
368:     database.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)');
369:   } catch (error: unknown) {
370:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
371:     console.warn(`[memory-retention-sweep] WAL checkpoint skipped: ${message}`);
372:   }
373: }
374: 
375: /**
376:  * Sweep expired governed memory rows.
377:  *
378:  * Rows are selected by normalized `datetime(delete_after) < datetime('now')`
379:  * to enforce the ISO timestamps persisted by governed ingest.
380:  */
381: export function runMemoryRetentionSweep(
382:   database: Database.Database,
383:   args: MemoryRetentionSweepArgs = {},
384: ): MemoryRetentionSweepResult {
385:   const startTime = Date.now();
386:   const dryRun = args.dryRun === true;
387:   const useSoftDeleteTombstones = isSoftDeleteTombstonesEnabled();
388: 
389:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_index')) {
390:     return {
391:       swept: 0,
392:       retained: 0,
393:       protectedCount: 0,
394:       dryRun,
395:       durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
396:       candidates: [],
397:       deletedIds: [],
398:       protectedIds: [],
399:       ledgerRecorded: null,
400:       tombstoneState: {
401:         usesPurgeablePartition: false,
402:         usingPurgeableIndex: false,
403:         candidateCount: 0,
404:         deletedCount: 0,
405:       },
406:       edgeState: {
407:         causalEdgesTablePresent: false,
408:         candidateEdgeCount: 0,
409:       },
410:     };
411:   }
412: 
413:   const candidates = selectExpiredRows(database, useSoftDeleteTombstones);
414:   const initialEdgeState = buildEdgeState(database, candidates);
415:   const feedbackRetention = isFeedbackRetentionLearningEnabled()
416:     ? buildFeedbackRetentionReport(database, candidates, dryRun, args.feedbackRetention)
417:     : null;
418: 
419:   if (dryRun || candidates.length === 0) {
420:     const totalRows = countRows(database);
421:     const dryRunProtectedIds = candidates
422:       .filter((candidate) => isProtectedFromRetentionDelete(candidate))
423:       .map((candidate) => candidate.id);
424:     return {
425:       swept: 0,
426:       retained: totalRows,
427:       protectedCount: dryRunProtectedIds.length,
428:       dryRun,
429:       durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
430:       candidates,
431:       deletedIds: [],
432:       protectedIds: dryRunProtectedIds,
433:       ledgerRecorded: null,
434:       tombstoneState: buildTombstoneState(database, candidates.length, 0, useSoftDeleteTombstones),
435:       edgeState: initialEdgeState,
436:       ...(feedbackRetention ? { feedbackRetention } : {}),
437:     };
438:   }
439: 
440:   if (feedbackRetention && (feedbackRetention.mode === 'shadow' || feedbackRetention.activeBlocked)) {
441:     const auditTx = database.transaction(() => {
442:       feedbackRetention.auditCount = recordFeedbackRetentionAudits(
443:         database,
444:         candidates,
445:         feedbackRetention,
446:         false,
447:       );
448:     });
449:     auditTx();
450: 
451:     return {
452:       swept: 0,
453:       retained: countRows(database),
454:       protectedCount: feedbackRetention.protectedIds.length,
455:       dryRun,
456:       durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
457:       candidates,
458:       deletedIds: [],
459:       protectedIds: feedbackRetention.protectedIds,
460:       extendedIds: [],
461:       ledgerRecorded: null,
462:       tombstoneState: buildTombstoneState(database, candidates.length, 0, useSoftDeleteTombstones),
463:       edgeState: initialEdgeState,
464:       feedbackRetention,
465:     };
466:   }
467: 
468:   initHistory(database);
469: 
470:   const deletedIds: number[] = [];
471:   const protectedIds: number[] = [];
472:   const extendedIds: number[] = [];
473:   let ledgerRecorded: boolean | null = null;
474: 
475:   const sweepTx = database.transaction(() => {
476:     for (const candidate of candidates) {
477:       // TOCTOU guard: skip rows un-expired by another writer since selection.
478:       if (!isStillExpired(database, candidate.id, useSoftDeleteTombstones)) {
479:         continue;
480:       }
481:       if (feedbackRetention) {
482:         const decision = feedbackRetention.decisions.find((entry) => entry.memoryId === candidate.id);
483:         if (!decision) {
484:           continue;
485:         }
486: 
487:         if (decision.decision === 'protect') {
488:           database.prepare('UPDATE memory_index SET delete_after = NULL WHERE id = ?').run(candidate.id);
489:           protectedIds.push(candidate.id);
490:           recordFeedbackRetentionAudit(database, candidate, decision, {
491:             mode: feedbackRetention.mode,
492:             applied: true,
493:             activeGatePassed: feedbackRetention.activeGatePassed,
494:           });
495:           feedbackRetention.auditCount += 1;
496:           continue;
497:         }
498: 
499:         if (decision.decision === 'extend') {
500:           database.prepare('UPDATE memory_index SET delete_after = ? WHERE id = ?').run(

168: /**

432:   }
433: }

435: /**
436:  * Merge two content strings by appending unique new lines.
437:  *
438:  * Splits both contents into lines, then appends lines from the new
439:  * content that are not present in the existing content.
440:  *
441:  * @param existing - The existing memory content
442:  * @param incoming - The new memory content
443:  * @returns The merged content string
444:  */
445: export function mergeContent(existing: string, incoming: string): string {
446:   if (!existing || existing.trim().length === 0) {
447:     return incoming;
448:   }
449:   if (!incoming || incoming.trim().length === 0) {
450:     return existing;
451:   }
452: 
453:   const existingLines = new Set(
454:     existing.split('\n').map(line => line.trim()).filter(line => line.length > 0)
455:   );
456: 
457:   const newLines = incoming
458:     .split('\n')
459:     .map(line => line.trim())
460:     .filter(line => line.length > 0 && !existingLines.has(line));
461: 
462:   if (newLines.length === 0) {
463:     return existing;
464:   }
465: 
466:   return existing + '\n\n<!-- Merged content -->\n' + newLines.join('\n');
467: }
468: 
469: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
470: // 7. CONFLICT OPERATION
471: 
472: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
473: /**
474:  * Resolve a conflict between highly similar memories (similarity 0.75-0.88).
475:  *
476:  * Preferred path (when caller provides a distinct new memory ID):
477:  * - Mark existing memory as deprecated (superseded)
478:  * - Create a 'supersedes' causal edge from new -> existing
479:  *
480:  * Legacy fallback (when no new ID is available):
481:  * - Update existing memory content/title in-place
482:  * - Skip edge creation (avoids self-referential edges)
483:  *
484:  * @param existingMemory - The existing memory being superseded
485:  * @param newMemory - The new memory replacing it
486:  * @param db - The database instance
487:  * @returns ConflictResult with supersede details
488:  */
489: export function executeConflict(
490:   existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
491:   newMemory: NewMemoryData,
492:   db: Database.Database
493: ): ConflictResult | ConflictAbortedResult {
494:   try {
495:     // Add causal 'supersedes' edge only when caller provides a distinct new ID.
496:     // Prevent self-referential supersedes edges (source == target).
497:     let edgeId: number | null = null;
498:     const baselineRow = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(existingMemory.id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
499:     if (!baselineRow || isArchivedRow(baselineRow)) {
500:       return buildConflictAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, 'conflict_stale_predecessor');
501:     }
502:     const predecessorSnapshot = capturePredecessorSnapshot(existingMemory, baselineRow);
503:     const hasDistinctNewId =
504:       typeof newMemory.id === 'number' &&
505:       Number.isFinite(newMemory.id) &&
506:       newMemory.id !== existingMemory.id;
507: 
508:     if (hasDistinctNewId) {
509:       const abortStatus = executeAtomicReconsolidationTxn(
510:         db,
511:         existingMemory,
512:         predecessorSnapshot,
513:         {
514:           mode: 'deprecate',
515:           apply: () => {
516:             const updateResult = db.prepare(`
517:               UPDATE memory_index
518:               SET importance_tier = 'deprecated',
519:                   updated_at = datetime('now')
520:               WHERE id = @id
521:                 AND importance_tier != 'deprecated'
522:                 AND ((content_hash = @contentHash) OR (content_hash IS NULL AND @contentHash IS NULL))
523:             `).run({
524:               id: existingMemory.id,
525:               contentHash: predecessorSnapshot.contentHash,
526:             }) as { changes: number };
527: 
528:             if (updateResult.changes === 0) {
529:               return false;
530:             }
531: 
532:             const sourceId = newMemory.id;
533:             const targetId = existingMemory.id;
534:             if (sourceId == null || targetId == null) {
535:               throw new Error('Reconsolidation source/target memory missing id — aborting');
536:             }
537:             edgeId = insertSupersedesEdge(db, sourceId, targetId);
538:             if (edgeId == null) {
539:               throw new Error(
540:                 `Failed to insert supersedes edge (${sourceId} -> ${targetId}) — aborting reconsolidation`
541:               );
542:             }
543:             return true;
544:           },
545:         },
546:       );
547:       if (abortStatus) {
548:         return buildConflictAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, abortStatus);
549:       }
550:     } else {
551:       // Atomic transaction: content + embedding + hash update together.
552:       const updatedHash = createHash('sha256').update(newMemory.content, 'utf-8').digest('hex');
553:       const abortStatus = executeAtomicReconsolidationTxn(
554:         db,
555:         existingMemory,
556:         predecessorSnapshot,
557:         {
558:           mode: 'content_update',
559:           apply: () => {
560:             const updateResult = db.prepare(`
561:               UPDATE memory_index
562:               SET content_text = ?,
563:                   title = ?,
564:                   content_hash = ?,
565:                   updated_at = datetime('now')
566:               WHERE id = ?
567:                 AND importance_tier != 'deprecated'
568:                 AND ((content_hash = ?) OR (content_hash IS NULL AND ? IS NULL))
569:             `).run(
570:               newMemory.content,
571:               newMemory.title,
572:               updatedHash,
573:               existingMemory.id,
574:               predecessorSnapshot.contentHash,
575:               predecessorSnapshot.contentHash,
576:             ) as { changes: number };
577:             if (updateResult.changes === 0) {
578:               return false;
579:             }
580: 
581:             if (newMemory.embedding) {
582:               const buffer = embeddingToBuffer(newMemory.embedding);
583:               db.prepare(
584:                 'UPDATE vec_memories SET embedding = ? WHERE rowid = ?'
585:               ).run(buffer, existingMemory.id);
586:             }
587:             return true;
588:           },
589:         },
590:       );
591:       if (abortStatus) {
592:         return buildConflictAbortResult(existingMemory, newMemory, abortStatus);
593:       }
594:     }
595: 
596:     return {
597:       action: 'conflict',
598:       existingMemoryId: existingMemory.id,
599:       newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? existingMemory.id,
600:       causalEdgeId: edgeId,
601:       similarity: existingMemory.similarity,
602:     };
603:   } catch (err: unknown) {
604:     const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
605:     throw new Error(`Conflict operation failed: ${message}`);
606:   }
607: }

613: /**

615:  *
616:  * The new memory is distinct enough from existing memories to be
617:  * stored as a separate entry.
618:  *
619:  * @param newMemory - The new memory to store
620:  * @param storeMemory - Callback to store the memory
621:  * @param topSimilarity - The highest similarity score found (null if no candidates)
622:  * @returns ComplementResult with storage details
623:  */
624: export function executeComplement(
625:   newMemory: NewMemoryData,
626:   storeMemory: StoreMemoryFn,
627:   topSimilarity: number | null
628: ): ComplementResult {
629:   const newMemoryId = storeMemory(newMemory);
630: 
631:   return {
632:     action: 'complement',
633:     newMemoryId,
634:     similarity: topSimilarity,
635:   };
636: }

639: // 9. RECONSOLIDATION ORCHESTRATOR
640: 
641: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
642: /** Options for the reconsolidation orchestrator */
643: export interface ReconsolidateOptions {
644:   findSimilar: FindSimilarFn;
645:   storeMemory: StoreMemoryFn;
646:   generateEmbedding?: GenerateEmbeddingFn | null;
647: }
648: 
649: /**
650:  * Orchestrate the full reconsolidation flow for a new memory.
651:  *
652:  * 1. Find top-3 most similar memories in the spec folder
653:  * 2. Determine action based on highest similarity score
654:  * 3. Execute the appropriate operation (merge/conflict/complement)
655:  *
656:  * When the feature flag is OFF, returns null (caller should use
657:  * normal store path).
658:  *
659:  * @param newMemory - The new memory to reconsolidate
660:  * @param db - The database instance
661:  * @param options - Callbacks for similarity search and storage
662:  * @returns ReconsolidationResult or null if feature is disabled
663:  */
664: export async function reconsolidate(
665:   newMemory: NewMemoryData,
666:   db: Database.Database,
667:   options: ReconsolidateOptions
668: ): Promise<ReconsolidationResult | null> {
669:   if (!isReconsolidationEnabled()) {
670:     return null;
671:   }
672: 
673:   const { findSimilar, storeMemory, generateEmbedding } = options;
674: 
675:   // Step 1: Find similar memories
676:   const similarMemories = findSimilarMemories(
677:     newMemory.embedding,
678:     newMemory.specFolder,
679:     findSimilar
680:   );
681: 
682:   // No existing memories: complement (new)
683:   if (similarMemories.length === 0) {
684:     // Do not persist in orchestrator complement path.
685:     // Caller owns canonical create flow (prevents duplicate writes).
686:     return {
687:       action: 'complement',
688:       newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
689:       similarity: null,
690:     };
691:   }
692: 
693:   // Step 2: Get the most similar memory and determine action
694:   const topMatch = similarMemories[0];
695:   const action = determineAction(topMatch.similarity);
696: 
697:   // Step 3: Execute action
698:   switch (action) {
699:     case 'merge':
700:       return executeMerge(topMatch, newMemory, db, generateEmbedding);
701: 
702:     case 'conflict':
703:       {
704:         let conflictMemory = newMemory;
705: 
706:         // F04-002: Wrap store + conflict in single transaction for atomicity
707:         // live-save path: materialize memory + supersede edge together
708:         try {
709:           const conflictTx = db.transaction(() => {
710:             if (conflictMemory.id === undefined) {
711:               const storedId = storeMemory(newMemory);
712:               if (
713:                 typeof storedId === 'number' &&
714:                 Number.isFinite(storedId) &&
715:                 storedId > 0 &&
716:                 storedId !== topMatch.id
717:               ) {
718:                 conflictMemory = { ...newMemory, id: storedId };
719:               }
720:             }
721:             return executeConflict(topMatch, conflictMemory, db);
722:           });
723:           return conflictTx();
724:         } catch (conflictErr: unknown) {
725:           // If storeMemory succeeded but executeConflict failed, clean up the orphan
726:           // Memory so we don't leave dangling rows with no supersedes edge.
727:           if (conflictMemory.id !== undefined && conflictMemory.id !== newMemory.id) {
728:             // Graph cleanup: Use delete_memory_from_database (includes deleteAncillaryMemoryRows)
729:             // instead of raw DELETE to clean lineage, projections, and graph residue.
730:             try {
731:               const deleted = delete_memory_from_database(db, conflictMemory.id);
732:               if (deleted) {
733:                 try {
734:                   recordHistory(
735:                     conflictMemory.id!,
736:                     'DELETE',
737:                     null,
738:                     null,
739:                     'mcp:reconsolidation_cleanup',
740:                     conflictMemory.specFolder ?? null,
741:                   );
742:                 } catch (_histErr: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
743:               }
744:             } catch (_error: unknown) {
745:               // Best-effort cleanup
746:             }
747:             console.warn('[reconsolidation] cleaned up orphan memory', conflictMemory.id, 'after executeConflict failure');
748:           }
749:           throw conflictErr;
750:         }
751:       }
752: 
753:     case 'complement':
754:       // Complement is a routing decision only; caller persists once.
755:       return {
756:         action: 'complement',
757:         newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
758:         similarity: topMatch.similarity,
759:       };
760: 
761:     default:
762:       // Exhaustive check
763:       return {
764:         action: 'complement',
765:         newMemoryId: newMemory.id ?? 0,
766:         similarity: topMatch.similarity,
767:       };
768:   }
769: }

931: /**
932:  * Re-run the shared compare-and-swap guards before applying a conflict-path
933:  * mutation so both transaction modes abort the same way on stale predecessors.
934:  */
935: function executeAtomicReconsolidationTxn(
936:   db: Database.Database,
937:   existingMemory: SimilarMemory,
938:   predecessorSnapshot: PredecessorSnapshot,
939:   operation: ReconsolidationOp,
940: ): ConflictAbortStatus | null {
941:   let abortStatus: ConflictAbortStatus | null = null;
942:   db.transaction(() => {
943:     const currentRow = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(existingMemory.id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
944:     if (!currentRow || isArchivedRow(currentRow)) {
945:       abortStatus = 'conflict_stale_predecessor';
946:       return;
947:     }
948:     if (hasScopeRetagged(predecessorSnapshot, currentRow)) {
949:       abortStatus = 'scope_retagged';
950:       return;
951:     }
952:     if (hasPredecessorChanged(predecessorSnapshot, currentRow)) {
953:       abortStatus = 'conflict_stale_predecessor';
954:       return;
955:     }
956: 
957:     if (!operation.apply()) {
958:       abortStatus = 'conflict_stale_predecessor';
959:     }
960:   })();
961: 
962:   return abortStatus;
963: }

979: function buildConflictAbortResult(

984:   const normalizedStatus = (() => {

987:       case 'scope_retagged':
988:         return status;

990:         return assertNever(status, 'conflict-abort-status');

992:   })();
993:   return {
994:     action: 'conflict',
995:     status: normalizedStatus,
996:     existingMemoryId: existingMemory.id,

1125:   const sourceAnchor = getMemoryAnchor(db, sourceMemoryId);

1128:   const values: unknown[] = [
1129:     String(sourceMemoryId),

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: N3-Lite Consolidation Engine
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Lightweight graph maintenance: contradiction scan, Hebbian
5: // Strengthening, staleness detection, edge bounds enforcement.
6: // Behind SPECKIT_CONSOLIDATION flag.
7: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
8: import { isConsolidationEnabled } from '../search/search-flags.js';
9: import {
10:   updateEdge,
11:   getStaleEdges,
12:   countEdgesForNode,
13:   isAutoEdgeCreator,
14:   MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE,
15:   MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH,
16:   MAX_STRENGTH_INCREASE_PER_CYCLE,
17:   STALENESS_THRESHOLD_DAYS,
18:   DECAY_STRENGTH_AMOUNT,
19:   DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS,
20: } from './causal-edges.js';
21: import type { CausalEdge } from './causal-edges.js';
22: 
23: // Feature catalog: Lightweight consolidation
24: 
25: 
26: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
27:    1. TYPES
28: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
29: 
30: /**
31:  * Describes the ContradictionPair shape.
32:  */
33: export interface ContradictionPair {
34:   memoryA: { id: number; title: string | null; content: string | null };
35:   memoryB: { id: number; title: string | null; content: string | null };
36:   similarity: number;
37:   conflictType: 'keyword_negation' | 'semantic_opposition';
38: }
39: 
40: /**
41:  * Describes the ContradictionCluster shape.
42:  */
43: export interface ContradictionCluster {
44:   /** The initially detected pair */
45:   seedPair: ContradictionPair;
46:   /** All cluster members (IDs of related memories) */
47:   members: number[];
48: }
49: 
50: /**
51:  * Describes the ConsolidationResult shape.
52:  */
53: export interface ConsolidationResult {
54:   contradictions: ContradictionCluster[];
55:   hebbian: { strengthened: number; decayed: number };
56:   stale: { flagged: number };
57:   edgeBounds: { rejected: number };
58: }
59: 
60: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
61:    2. CONSTANTS
62: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
63: 
64: /** Cosine similarity threshold for contradiction candidates */
65: const CONTRADICTION_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.85;
66: 
67: /** Consolidation cadence for runtime hook (weekly batch semantics) */
68: const CONSOLIDATION_INTERVAL_DAYS = 7;
69: 
70: /** Negation keywords for lightweight contradiction heuristic */
71: const NEGATION_KEYWORDS = [
72:   'not', 'never', 'no longer', 'instead', 'removed', 'deprecated',
73:   'replaced', 'incorrect', 'wrong', 'outdated', 'contrary', 'opposite',
74:   "don't", "doesn't", "shouldn't", "won't", "can't", "isn't", "aren't",
75: ];
76: 
77: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
78:    CONTRADICTION SCAN
79: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
80: 
81: /**
82:  * Find potential contradictions by:
83:  * 1. Candidate generation — high cosine similarity pairs (>0.85)
84:  * 2. Conflict check — keyword negation heuristic
85:  *
86:  * Returns pairs that are both semantically similar AND contain
87:  * negation patterns suggesting conflicting information.
88:  */
89: export function scanContradictions(
90:   database: Database.Database,
91: ): ContradictionPair[] {
92:   const pairs: ContradictionPair[] = [];
93: 
94:   try {
95:     // Query high-similarity pairs from vec_memories using sqlite-vec
96:     // Fall back to content-based comparison if vec not available
97:     const hasSqliteVec = checkSqliteVecAvailable(database);
98: 
99:     if (hasSqliteVec) {
100:       pairs.push(...scanContradictionsVector(database));
101:     } else {
102:       pairs.push(...scanContradictionsHeuristic(database));
103:     }
104:   } catch (error: unknown) {
105:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
106:     console.warn(`[consolidation] scanContradictions error: ${msg}`);
107:   }
108: 
109:   return pairs;
110: }
111: 
112: function checkSqliteVecAvailable(database: Database.Database): boolean {
113:   try {
114:     const result = (database.prepare(
115:       "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='vec_memories'"
116:     ) as Database.Statement).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
117:     return !!result;
118:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
119:     return false;
120:   }
121: }
122: 
123: /**
124:  * Vector-based contradiction scan using sqlite-vec cosine similarity.
125:  */
126: function scanContradictionsVector(
127:   database: Database.Database,
128: ): ContradictionPair[] {
129:   const pairs: ContradictionPair[] = [];
130: 
131:   // Get all non-deprecated, non-chunk memories with embeddings
132:   const memories = (database.prepare(`
133:     SELECT m.id, m.title, m.content_text, v.embedding
134:     FROM memory_index m
135:     JOIN vec_memories v ON v.rowid = m.id
136:     WHERE m.importance_tier != 'deprecated'
137:       AND m.parent_id IS NULL
138:       AND m.content_text IS NOT NULL
139:       AND length(m.content_text) > 50
140:     ORDER BY m.id
141:     LIMIT 500
142:   `) as Database.Statement).all() as Array<{
143:     id: number;
144:     title: string | null;
145:     content_text: string | null;
146:     embedding: Buffer;
147:   }>;
148: 
149:   // O(n^2) pairwise scan capped at 500 memories (up to 124,750 pairs).
150:   // A 5-second deadline prevents runaway CPU when embeddings are large or the
151:   // Machine is slow, keeping the consolidation cycle non-blocking.
152:   const deadline = Date.now() + 5000;
153: 
154:   // Pairwise similarity check (O(n^2) but capped at 500 memories)
155:   for (let i = 0; i < memories.length; i++) {
156:     for (let j = i + 1; j < memories.length; j++) {
157:       if (Date.now() > deadline) {
158:         console.warn('[consolidation] Contradiction scan timed out after 5s');
159:         return pairs;
160:       }
161:       const similarity = computeCosineSimilarity(
162:         new Float32Array(memories[i].embedding.buffer, memories[i].embedding.byteOffset, memories[i].embedding.byteLength / 4),
163:         new Float32Array(memories[j].embedding.buffer, memories[j].embedding.byteOffset, memories[j].embedding.byteLength / 4),
164:       );
165: 
166:       if (similarity >= CONTRADICTION_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD) {
167:         const contentA = memories[i].content_text || '';
168:         const contentB = memories[j].content_text || '';
169: 
170:         if (hasNegationConflict(contentA, contentB)) {
171:           pairs.push({
172:             memoryA: { id: memories[i].id, title: memories[i].title, content: contentA },
173:             memoryB: { id: memories[j].id, title: memories[j].title, content: contentB },
174:             similarity,
175:             conflictType: 'keyword_negation',
176:           });
177:         }
178:       }
179:     }
180:   }
181: 
182:   return pairs;
183: }
184: 
185: /**
186:  * Heuristic-only contradiction scan when vector similarity is unavailable.
187:  * Uses title and content text comparison.
188:  */
189: function scanContradictionsHeuristic(
190:   database: Database.Database,
191: ): ContradictionPair[] {
192:   const pairs: ContradictionPair[] = [];
193: 
194:   const memories = (database.prepare(`
195:     SELECT id, title, content_text
196:     FROM memory_index
197:     WHERE importance_tier != 'deprecated'
198:       AND parent_id IS NULL
199:       AND content_text IS NOT NULL
200:       AND length(content_text) > 50
201:     ORDER BY id
202:     LIMIT 200
203:   `) as Database.Statement).all() as Array<{
204:     id: number;
205:     title: string | null;
206:     content_text: string | null;
207:   }>;
208: 
209:   // Same 5-second timeout guard as the vector-based scan to prevent
210:   // Unbounded CPU usage in the heuristic O(n^2) fallback path.
211:   const deadline = Date.now() + 5000;
212: 
213:   // Simple word-overlap heuristic for candidate generation
214:   for (let i = 0; i < memories.length; i++) {
215:     for (let j = i + 1; j < memories.length; j++) {
216:       if (Date.now() > deadline) {
217:         console.warn('[consolidation] Contradiction scan (heuristic) timed out after 5s');
218:         return pairs;
219:       }
220:       const contentA = memories[i].content_text || '';
221:       const contentB = memories[j].content_text || '';
222: 
223:       const overlap = computeWordOverlap(contentA, contentB);
224:       if (overlap >= CONTRADICTION_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD && hasNegationConflict(contentA, contentB)) {
225:         pairs.push({
226:           memoryA: { id: memories[i].id, title: memories[i].title, content: contentA },
227:           memoryB: { id: memories[j].id, title: memories[j].title, content: contentB },
228:           similarity: overlap,
229:           conflictType: 'keyword_negation',
230:         });
231:       }
232:     }
233:   }
234: 
235:   return pairs;
236: }
237: 
238: /**
239:  * Check if two texts contain negation patterns suggesting contradiction.
240:  */
241: export function hasNegationConflict(textA: string, textB: string): boolean {
242:   const lowerA = textA.toLowerCase();
243:   const lowerB = textB.toLowerCase();
244: 
245:   for (const keyword of NEGATION_KEYWORDS) {
246:     const inA = lowerA.includes(keyword);
247:     const inB = lowerB.includes(keyword);
248: 
249:     // One has negation, the other doesn't → potential contradiction
250:     if (inA !== inB) return true;
251:   }
252: 
253:   return false;
254: }
255: 
256: function computeCosineSimilarity(a: Float32Array, b: Float32Array): number {
257:   if (a.length !== b.length || a.length === 0) return 0;
258:   let dot = 0, normA = 0, normB = 0;
259:   for (let i = 0; i < a.length; i++) {
260:     dot += a[i] * b[i];
261:     normA += a[i] * a[i];
262:     normB += b[i] * b[i];
263:   }
264:   const denom = Math.sqrt(normA) * Math.sqrt(normB);
265:   return denom > 0 ? dot / denom : 0;
266: }
267: 
268: function computeWordOverlap(textA: string, textB: string): number {
269:   const wordsA = new Set(textA.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/).filter(w => w.length > 3));
270:   const wordsB = new Set(textB.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/).filter(w => w.length > 3));
271:   if (wordsA.size === 0 || wordsB.size === 0) return 0;
272: 
273:   let overlap = 0;
274:   for (const w of wordsA) {
275:     if (wordsB.has(w)) overlap++;
276:   }
277:   return overlap / Math.max(wordsA.size, wordsB.size);
278: }
279: 
280: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
281:    CONTRADICTION CLUSTER SURFACING
282: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
283: 
284: /**
285:  * Surface all members of a contradiction cluster.
286:  * When a contradiction pair is detected, find ALL related memories
287:  * (via causal edges) to surface the full context for resolution.
288:  */
289: export function buildContradictionClusters(
290:   database: Database.Database,
291:   pairs: ContradictionPair[],
292: ): ContradictionCluster[] {
293:   const clusters: ContradictionCluster[] = [];
294: 
295:   for (const pair of pairs) {
296:     const memberIds = new Set<number>([pair.memoryA.id, pair.memoryB.id]);
297: 
298:     // Expand cluster via causal edges (1-hop neighbors)
299:     for (const seedId of [pair.memoryA.id, pair.memoryB.id]) {
300:       try {
301:         const neighbors = (database.prepare(`
302:           SELECT CAST(target_id AS INTEGER) as neighbor_id FROM causal_edges WHERE source_id = ?
303:           UNION
304:           SELECT CAST(source_id AS INTEGER) as neighbor_id FROM causal_edges WHERE target_id = ?
305:         `) as Database.Statement).all(String(seedId), String(seedId)) as Array<{ neighbor_id: number }>;
306: 
307:         for (const n of neighbors) {
308:           if (!isNaN(n.neighbor_id)) {
309:             memberIds.add(n.neighbor_id);
310:           }
311:         }
312:       } catch (err: unknown) {
313:         const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
314:         console.warn('[consolidation] cluster expansion error:', message);
315:       }
316:     }
317: 
318:     clusters.push({
319:       seedPair: pair,
320:       members: Array.from(memberIds),
321:     });
322:   }
323: 
324:   return clusters;
325: }
326: 
327: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
328:    HEBBIAN EDGE STRENGTHENING
329: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
330: 
331: /**
332:  * Hebbian strengthening: increase edge strength for recently co-accessed edges.
333:  * +0.05 per cycle, capped at MAX_STRENGTH_INCREASE_PER_CYCLE.
334:  * 30-day decay: edges not accessed in 30 days lose 0.1 strength.
335:  *
336:  * All weight changes are logged to weight_history via updateEdge().
337:  */
338: export function runHebbianCycle(database: Database.Database): { strengthened: number; decayed: number } {
339:   let strengthened = 0;
340:   let decayed = 0;
341: 
342:   try {
343:     // Wrap all DB operations in a single transaction for atomicity
344:     database.transaction(() => {
345:       // Strengthen: edges accessed in the last cycle period (7 days)
346:       const recentEdges = (database.prepare(`
347:         SELECT id, strength, last_accessed, created_by FROM causal_edges
348:         WHERE last_accessed IS NOT NULL
349:           AND last_accessed > datetime('now', '-7 days')
350:           AND strength < 1.0
351:       `) as Database.Statement).all() as CausalEdge[];
352: 
353:       for (const edge of recentEdges) {
354:         const increase = Math.min(MAX_STRENGTH_INCREASE_PER_CYCLE, 1.0 - edge.strength);
355:         if (increase > 0) {
356:           const newStrength = Math.min(1.0, edge.strength + increase);
357:           // Auto edges (incl. namespaced auto-* creators) cannot exceed MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH
358:           const cappedStrength = isAutoEdgeCreator(edge.created_by)
359:             ? Math.min(newStrength, MAX_AUTO_STRENGTH)
360:             : newStrength;
361: 
362:           if (cappedStrength > edge.strength) {
363:             updateEdge(edge.id, { strength: cappedStrength }, 'hebbian', 'hebbian-strengthening');
364:             strengthened++;
365:           }
366:         }
367:       }
368: 
369:       // Decay: edges not accessed in DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS
370:       const staleDecayEdges = (database.prepare(`
371:         SELECT id, strength, last_accessed, created_by FROM causal_edges
372:         WHERE (last_accessed IS NULL AND extracted_at < datetime('now', '-' || ? || ' days'))
373:            OR (last_accessed IS NOT NULL AND last_accessed < datetime('now', '-' || ? || ' days'))
374:       `) as Database.Statement).all(DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS, DECAY_PERIOD_DAYS) as CausalEdge[];
375: 
376:       for (const edge of staleDecayEdges) {
377:         const newStrength = Math.max(0, edge.strength - DECAY_STRENGTH_AMOUNT);
378:         if (newStrength < edge.strength) {
379:           updateEdge(edge.id, { strength: newStrength }, 'hebbian', 'decay-30-day');
380:           decayed++;
381:         }
382:       }
383:     })();
384:   } catch (error: unknown) {
385:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
386:     console.warn(`[consolidation] runHebbianCycle error: ${msg}`);
387:   }
388: 
389:   return { strengthened, decayed };
390: }
391: 
392: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
393:    STALENESS DETECTION
394: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
395: 
396: /**
397:  * Detect stale edges (not accessed in 90+ days).
398:  * Flags them for review without deletion.
399:  */
400: export function detectStaleEdges(_database: Database.Database): CausalEdge[] {
401:   return getStaleEdges(STALENESS_THRESHOLD_DAYS);
402: }
403: 
404: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
405:    EDGE BOUNDS ENFORCEMENT
406: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
407: 
408: /**
409:  * Enforce edge bounds for a node:
410:  * - MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE = 20
411:  * - Auto edges capped at strength = 0.5
412:  *
413:  * Returns count of edges that would be rejected.
414:  */
415: export function checkEdgeBounds(
416:   nodeId: string,
417: ): { currentCount: number; maxAllowed: number; canAddAuto: boolean } {
418:   const currentCount = countEdgesForNode(nodeId);
419:   return {
420:     currentCount,
421:     maxAllowed: MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE,
422:     canAddAuto: currentCount < MAX_EDGES_PER_NODE,
423:   };
424: }
425: 
426: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
427:    8. FULL CONSOLIDATION CYCLE
428: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
429: 
430: /**
431:  * Run a full N3-lite consolidation cycle:
432:  * 1. Contradiction scan
433:  * 2. Hebbian strengthening + decay
434:  * 3. Staleness detection
435:  * 4. Edge bounds check
436:  *
437:  * Designed to run as a weekly batch job.
438:  */
439: export function runConsolidationCycle(database: Database.Database): ConsolidationResult {
440:   // Contradiction scan + cluster surfacing
441:   const contradictionPairs = scanContradictions(database);
442:   const contradictions = buildContradictionClusters(database, contradictionPairs);
443: 
444:   // Hebbian strengthening + decay
445:   const hebbian = runHebbianCycle(database);
446: 
447:   // Staleness detection
448:   const staleEdges = detectStaleEdges(database);
449: 
450:   // Edge bounds — check counts (enforcement happens at insertEdge)
451:   let rejectedCount = 0;
452:   try {
453:     const nodes = (database.prepare(`
454:       SELECT DISTINCT node_id FROM (
455:         SELECT source_id AS node_id FROM causal_edges
456:         UNION
457:         SELECT target_id AS node_id FROM causal_edges
458:       )
459:     `) as Database.Statement).all() as Array<{ node_id: string }>;
460: 
461:     for (const { node_id } of nodes) {
462:       const bounds = checkEdgeBounds(node_id);
463:       if (!bounds.canAddAuto) rejectedCount++;
464:     }
465:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
466:     // Best-effort bounds check
467:   }
468: 
469:   return {
470:     contradictions,
471:     hebbian,
472:     stale: { flagged: staleEdges.length },
473:     edgeBounds: { rejected: rejectedCount },
474:   };
475: }
476: 
477: /**
478:  * Runtime gate for N3-lite consolidation.
479:  * Returns null when consolidation is disabled.
480:  */
481: export function runConsolidationCycleIfEnabled(
482:   database: Database.Database,
483: ): ConsolidationResult | null {
484:   if (!isConsolidationEnabled()) return null;
485: 
486:   let beganImmediate = false;
487:   try {
488:     if (!database.inTransaction) {
489:       database.exec('BEGIN IMMEDIATE');
490:       beganImmediate = true;
491:     }
492: 
493:     (database.prepare(`
494:       CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS consolidation_state (
495:         id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK(id = 1),
496:         last_run_at TEXT
497:       )
498:     `) as Database.Statement).run();
499: 
500:     const row = (database.prepare(
501:       'SELECT last_run_at FROM consolidation_state WHERE id = 1'
502:     ) as Database.Statement).get() as { last_run_at: string | null } | undefined;
503: 
504:     if (row?.last_run_at) {
505:       const due = (database.prepare(`
506:         SELECT CASE
507:           WHEN ? <= datetime('now', '-' || ? || ' days') THEN 1
508:           ELSE 0
509:         END AS is_due
510:       `) as Database.Statement).get(row.last_run_at, CONSOLIDATION_INTERVAL_DAYS) as {
511:         is_due: number;
512:       };
513: 
514:       if (due.is_due !== 1) {
515:         if (beganImmediate) {
516:           database.exec('COMMIT');
517:           beganImmediate = false;
518:         }
519:         return null;
520:       }
521:     }
522: 
523:     const result = runConsolidationCycle(database);
524: 
525:     (database.prepare(`
526:       INSERT INTO consolidation_state (id, last_run_at)
527:       VALUES (1, datetime('now'))
528:       ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET last_run_at = datetime('now')
529:     `) as Database.Statement).run();
530: 
531:     if (beganImmediate) {
532:       database.exec('COMMIT');
533:       beganImmediate = false;
534:     }
535: 
536:     return result;
537:   } catch (err: unknown) {
538:     if (beganImmediate) {
539:       try {
540:         database.exec('ROLLBACK');
541:       } catch {
542:         // Ignore rollback errors after failed BEGIN/COMMIT paths.
543:       }
544:     }
545:     // Fail-closed: broken bookkeeping must not cause unbounded cycle runs
546:     const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
547:     console.warn('[consolidation] cadence bookkeeping error:', message);
548:     return null;
549:   }
550: }
551: 
552: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
553:    9. EXPORTS
554: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
555: 
556: export {
557:   CONTRADICTION_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD,
558: };

1651:         restoreContext: { checkpointSpecFolder, scope: normalizedScope },
1652:       });
1653:       return;
1654:     }
1655: 
1656:     if (memoryIds.length > 0) {
1657:       sweepCausalEdges(database, {
1658:         memoryIds,
1659:         reason: 'checkpoint restore scoped causal cleanup',
1660:         command: 'checkpoints.clearTableForRestoreScope',
1661:         restoreContext: { checkpointSpecFolder, scope: normalizedScope },
1662:       });
1663:     }
1664:     return;
1665:   }
1666: 
1667:   if (!checkpointSpecFolder && !hasScope && allowFullTableFallback) {
1668:     clearTable(database, tableName);
1669:     return;
1670:   }
1671: 
1672:   const columns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, tableName));
1673:   const { clauses: scopedClauses, params: scopedParams } = buildRestoreScopeDeleteWhere(
1674:     columns,
1675:     checkpointSpecFolder,
1676:     normalizedScope,
1677:   );
1678: 
1679:   if (tableName === 'memory_index') {
1680:     if (scopedClauses.length > 0) {
1681:       database.prepare(`DELETE FROM memory_index WHERE ${scopedClauses.join(' AND ')}`).run(...scopedParams);
1682:     }
1683:     return;
1684:   }
1685: 
1686:   if (tableName === 'vec_memories') {
1687:     deleteRowsByIds(database, 'vec_memories', 'rowid', memoryIds);
1688:     return;
1689:   }
1690: 
1691:   if (tableName === 'memory_corrections') {
1692:     if (memoryIds.length === 0) {
1693:       return;
1694:     }
1695:     deleteRowsByIds(database, tableName, 'original_memory_id', memoryIds);
1696:     deleteRowsByIds(database, tableName, 'correction_memory_id', memoryIds);
1697:     return;
1698:   }
1699: 
1700:   if (tableName === 'weight_history') {
1701:     deleteRowsByIds(database, tableName, 'edge_id', edgeIds);
1702:     return;
1703:   }
1704: 
1705:   if (scopedClauses.length > 0) {
1706:     database.prepare(`DELETE FROM ${tableName} WHERE ${scopedClauses.join(' AND ')}`).run(...scopedParams);
1707:     return;
1708:   }
1709: 
1710:   if (columns.has('spec_folder')) {
1711:     database.prepare(`DELETE FROM ${tableName} WHERE spec_folder = ?`).run(checkpointSpecFolder);
1712:     return;
1713:   }
1714: 
1715:   if (memoryIds.length > 0 && columns.has('memory_id')) {
1716:     deleteRowsByIds(database, tableName, 'memory_id', memoryIds);
1717:     return;
1718:   }
1719: 
1720:   if (!hasScope && allowFullTableFallback) {
1721:     clearTable(database, tableName);
1722:   }
1723: }

1766: function restoreMergeTableAtomically(
1767:   database: Database.Database,
1768:   tableName: string,
1769:   tableSnapshot: TableSnapshot,
1770:   options: {
1771:     checkpointSpecFolder: string | null;
1772:     memoryIds: number[];
1773:     edgeIds: number[];
1774:     scope: ScopeContext;
1775:   },
1776: ): { restoredCount: number; error?: string } {
1777:   const savepointName = `checkpoint_merge_${tableName.replace(/[^a-z0-9_]/gi, '_')}`;
1778: 
1779:   database.exec(`SAVEPOINT ${savepointName}`);
1780:   try {
1781:     clearTableForRestoreScope(database, tableName, {
1782:       ...options,
1783:       allowFullTableFallback: false,
1784:     });
1785:     const restoredCount = restoreGenericTable(database, tableName, tableSnapshot);
1786:     database.exec(`RELEASE SAVEPOINT ${savepointName}`);
1787:     return { restoredCount };
1788:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1789:     const msg = toErrorMessage(error);
1790:     try {
1791:       database.exec(`ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT ${savepointName}`);
1792:     } finally {
1793:       try {
1794:         database.exec(`RELEASE SAVEPOINT ${savepointName}`);
1795:       } catch {
1796:         // Ignore follow-up release errors after rollback.
1797:       }
1798:     }
1799:     return {
1800:       restoredCount: 0,
1801:       error: `${tableName}: merge restore rolled back after pre-clear because reinsertion failed: ${msg}`,
1802:     };
1803:   }
1804: }

1853: function restoreGenericTable(
1854:   database: Database.Database,
1855:   tableName: string,
1856:   tableSnapshot: TableSnapshot,
1857: ): number {
1858:   if (!tableExists(database, tableName)) {
1859:     return 0;
1860:   }
1861: 
1862:   const columns = tableSnapshot.columns.length > 0
1863:     ? tableSnapshot.columns
1864:     : Object.keys(tableSnapshot.rows[0] ?? {});
1865:   if (columns.length === 0 || tableSnapshot.rows.length === 0) {
1866:     return 0;
1867:   }
1868: 
1869:   const placeholders = columns.map(() => '?').join(', ');
1870:   const insertVerb = tableName === 'vec_memories' ? 'INSERT' : 'INSERT OR REPLACE';
1871:   const insertStmt = database.prepare(`
1872:     ${insertVerb} INTO ${tableName} (${columns.join(', ')})
1873:     VALUES (${placeholders})
1874:   `) as Database.Statement;
1875:   const deleteVecById = tableName === 'vec_memories'
1876:     ? database.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?') as Database.Statement
1877:     : null;
1878: 
1879:   let restored = 0;
1880:   for (const row of tableSnapshot.rows) {
1881:     const values = columns.map((column) => deserializeSnapshotValue(row[column]));
1882:     if (deleteVecById) {
1883:       deleteVecById.run(row.rowid);
1884:     }
1885:     insertStmt.run(...values);
1886:     restored++;
1887:   }
1888:   return restored;
1889: }

1895: function runDerivedArtifactRebuilds(
1896:   database: Database.Database,
1897:   options: RunDerivedArtifactRebuildOptions = {},
1898: ): DerivedRebuildSummary {
1899:   const hasMemoryParentId = tableHasColumn(database, 'memory_index', 'parent_id');
1900:   const checkpointSpecFolder = options.specFolder ?? null;
1901:   const actor = options.actor ?? 'mcp:checkpoint_restore';
1902:   const logContext = options.logContext ?? 'derived-artifact';
1903: 
1904:   const rebuildSteps: Array<{ name: string; deps: string[]; run: () => void }> = [
1905:     {
1906:       name: 'auto-entities',
1907:       deps: ['lineage-backfill'],
1908:       run: () => rebuildAutoEntities(database, { specFolder: checkpointSpecFolder ?? undefined }),
1909:     },
1910:     {
1911:       name: 'degree-snapshots',
1912:       deps: ['lineage-backfill'],
1913:       run: () => snapshotDegrees(database),
1914:     },
1915:     {
1916:       name: 'community-artifacts',
1917:       deps: ['degree-snapshots'],
1918:       run: () => {
1919:         const communities = detectCommunities(database);
1920:         storeCommunities(database, communities);
1921:         storeCommunityAssignments(database, communities);
1922:         generateCommunitySummaries(database, communities);
1923:       },
1924:     },
1925:     {
1926:       name: 'fts-rebuild',
1927:       deps: ['lineage-backfill', 'auto-entities'],
1928:       run: () => {
1929:         if (tableExists(database, 'memory_fts')) {
1930:           database.exec(`INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')`);
1931:         }
1932:       },
1933:     },
1934:   ];
1935: 
1936:   if (hasMemoryParentId) {
1937:     rebuildSteps.unshift({
1938:       name: 'lineage-backfill',
1939:       deps: [],
1940:       run: () => runLineageBackfill(database, { actor }),
1941:     });
1942:   }
1943: 
1944:   const completed = new Set<string>();
1945:   const failed: DerivedRebuildFailure[] = [];
1946:   const skipped: DerivedRebuildSkipped[] = [];
1947: 
1948:   for (const { name, deps, run } of rebuildSteps) {
1949:     const missingDeps = deps.filter((dependency) => !completed.has(dependency));
1950:     if (missingDeps.length > 0) {
1951:       const reason = `dependencies did not complete: ${missingDeps.join(', ')}`;
1952:       skipped.push({ name, reason });
1953:       console.warn(
1954:         `[checkpoints] Skipping ${logContext} rebuild "${name}" because ${reason}`,
1955:       );
1956:       continue;
1957:     }
1958: 
1959:     try {
1960:       run();
1961:       completed.add(name);
1962:     } catch (err: unknown) {
1963:       const error = toErrorMessage(err);
1964:       failed.push({ name, error });
1965:       console.warn(`[checkpoints] ${logContext} rebuild "${name}" failed (non-fatal): ${error}`);
1966:     }
1967:   }
1968: 
1969:   const summary: DerivedRebuildSummary = {
1970:     completed: [...completed],
1971:     failed,
1972:     skipped,
1973:   };
1974: 
1975:   console.warn(
1976:     `[checkpoints] ${logContext} rebuild summary: completed=${summary.completed.join(', ') || 'none'}; failed=${summary.failed.map((entry) => entry.name).join(', ') || 'none'}; skipped=${summary.skipped.map((entry) => entry.name).join(', ') || 'none'}`,
1977:   );
1978: 
1979:   return summary;
1980: }

2047:     if (column === 'stability') return 1.0;
2048:     if (column === 'difficulty') return 5.0;

2056:   if (column === 'stability') {
2057:     return Number.isFinite(value) ? value : 1.0;

2059:   if (column === 'difficulty') {
